title: The Mad Shrine
lines: You hug the weeping stone of the cavern wall and step off the ledge into a cyclopean chamber of twisted, non-Euclidean geometry. The architecture is a nightmare of acute angles and impossible, nauseating curves that sear your retinas and defy the laws of earthly construction.
In the geometric center of the room sits a monolithic altar of porous, green stone that feels warm and slightly elastic to the touch, pulsing with a slow, tectonic rhythm.

Engraved deeply into the stone beneath a series of profane, shifting symbols is a single sentence carved in an archaic script: 'He that sleeps will never die, and with strange aeons even death may die.'
Behind the altar, a jagged, swirling tear in the very fabric of reality hums with a cold, absolute intensity—an amethyst light that reveals the chaotic, writhing 'under-structure' of the world.

options.desc: Stand and try to decipher the profane, shifting symbols
Approach the jagged, swirling amethyst tear in reality
